Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Moss is a pixel canvas where every brush is a tiny program 107 upvotes, 10 comments
  2. The Rust Calling Convention We Deserve 19 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. NanoGPT Slowrun: Language Modeling with Limited Data, Infinite Compute 79 upvotes, 9 comments
  4. Faster C software with Dynamic Feature Detection 29 upvotes, 2 comments
  5. My Favorite 39C3 Talks 24 upvotes, 2 comments
  6. An interactive map of Flock Cams 316 upvotes, 82 comments
  7. Qwen3.5 Fine-Tuning Guide – Unsloth Documentation 211 upvotes, 53 comments
  8. Libre Solar – Open Hardware for Renewable Energy 168 upvotes, 47 comments
  9. MyFirst Kids Watch Hacked. Access to Camera and Microphone 87 upvotes, 21 comments
  10. The Space Race's Forgotten Theme Park 15 upvotes, 1 comments
  11. Building a New Flash 26 upvotes, 4 comments
  12. Google ends its 30 percent app store fee and welcomes third-party app stores 104 upvotes, 30 comments
  13. “It turns out” (2010) 200 upvotes, 68 comments
  14. Something is afoot in the land of Qwen 382 upvotes, 190 comments
  15. RFC 9849. TLS Encrypted Client Hello 254 upvotes, 122 comments
  16. The View from RSS 13 upvotes, 2 comments
  17. Nobody Gets Promoted for Simplicity 752 upvotes, 433 comments
  18. Agentic Engineering Patterns 457 upvotes, 257 comments
  19. Humans 40k yrs ago developed a system of conventional signs 11 upvotes, 2 comments
  20. Glaze by Raycast 162 upvotes, 96 comments
  21. Data Has Weight but Only on SSDs 34 upvotes, 15 comments
  22. Emails to Outlook.com rejected due to a fault or overzealous blocking rules 116 upvotes, 70 comments
  23. Raspberry Pi Pico as AM Radio Transmitter 43 upvotes, 21 comments
  24. Making Firefox's right-click not suck with about:config 186 upvotes, 122 comments
  25. Does that use a lot of energy? 50 upvotes, 31 comments
  26. TikTok will not introduce end-to-end encryption, saying it makes users less safe 386 upvotes, 370 comments
  27. Was Windows 1.0's lack of overlapping windows a legal or a technical matter? 10 upvotes, 4 comments
  28. MacBook Neo 1235 upvotes, 1589 comments
  29. Father claims Google's AI product fuelled son's delusional spiral 105 upvotes, 115 comments
